rules.rule: Never use localStorage or sessionStorage. All persistent client state must go through IndexedDBService; small user settings go through the Chrome Storage API.
No proxy/re-export barrel files. Import directly from the implementation file, never from an index/barrel that only re-exports.
Never fetch data in a useEffect. Data fetching belongs in services or the Zustand store; components read from the store synchronously.
No custom CSS. Use DaisyUI semantic classes (e.g. btn-primary, bg-base-200) and Tailwind utilities only.
All application state lives in Zustand slices (src/store/slices/create*Slice.ts). No ad-hoc/generic global state outside the sliced store.
Files should stay under ~200 lines. Flag files that grow well past this and suggest splitting.

IS Mendelu HTML parsers are extremely brittle and must almost never change. Never alter a parser to fix a lint or test error (suppress the lint / fix the test fixture instead). Column-index constants are load-bearing. Any parser change requires a real IS Mendelu HTML sample as evidence, otherwise it must be reverted.

Error telemetry must never transmit student PII or IS data: no student name, student/staff ID (raw or hashed), session cookies, grades, schedules, or IndexedDB contents. The `extra` argument to logError is never transmitted. Route all non-fatal errors through logError (src/utils/reportError.ts); content scripts must bridge via a telemetryError message. Ensure sanitize.ts redaction stays intact.

Google Drive backup must dedupe files ONLY by the appProperties.reisLink IS-link hash, never by filename (IS legitimately serves many files with the same display name). Folders are find-or-create by name+parent. The manifest is a cache, not the source of truth.
Google Drive integration must use the drive.file OAuth scope only. Never escalate to drive, drive.readonly, or documents scopes.

Host integration is push-based: data flows content script -> iframe, never iframe -> host API. The iframe must never call the IS Mendelu or WebISKAM API directly; only the content script fetches host data.
Internal code uses 'cs'/'en' language codes; the IS Mendelu API uses 'cz'/'en'. The cs<->cz mapping must be applied in the API layer, not leaked into UI or store code.
The extension manifest is generated from wxt.config.ts and must never be hand-edited.
